THIS STORM BEGAN WITH TORNADIC PROPERTIES AT 7 PM CDT 9 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GARDEN CITY. THE CHASERS SPOTTED THE STORM ORIGINALLY TRACKING EAST, THEN SHIFTED SOUTHEAST, AND THEN SOUTH. THESE CHASERS WITNESSED 6 TO 8 DIFFERENT TOUCHDOWNS RANGING FROM 5 MILES SOUTH OF GARDEN CITY TO 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF PLYMEL ALONG US HIGHWAY 83. NUMEROUS POWER POLES WERE DOWNED ALONG US 83 FROM NEAR PLYMEL TO 8 MILES NORTH OF SUBLETTE.
TORNADO LASTED ABOUT A MINUTE.

THE CITIZEN ALSO REPORTED HOLES IN SIDING AS WELL AS SOME BROKEN SKYLIGHTS FROM NEIGHBORS HOME AND NOTED THAT ROOF WOULD LIKELY NEED TO BE REPLACED.
THE TOP WAS BROKEN FROM TREE THREE INCHES IN DIAMETER. HAIL UP TO 3/4 INCHES IN DIAMETER ALSO OCCURRED.

A 2 TO 3 MILE WIDE SWATH OF AT LEAST GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL ENTERED THE NW CORNER OF SEWARD COUNTY AND CONTINUED TO THE OKLAHOMA BORDER SOUTHWEST OF LIBERAL AT 1047 PM. CROPS WERE DESTROYED BY THE HAIL THAT WAS ACCOMPANIED BY 70 TO 85 MPH WINDS.
SEVERAL 5 TO 6 INCH TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN.
SEVERAL TWO FOOT IN DIAMETER TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.

A THUNDERSTORM ENTERED NW SEWARD COUNTY AT 1010 PM AND MOVED INTO OKLAHOMA AT 1047 PM SOUTHWEST OF LIBERAL. WIND SPEEDS OF 70 TO 85 MPH OVERTURNED AT LEAST THREE PIVOT IRRIGATION SPRINKLERS, BROKE 2 FOOT DIAMETER TREE LIMBS AND REMOVED AN 80 FOOT SECTION OF A LARGE STEEL BUILDING. OTHER SMALLER OUT-BUILDNGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. THE WIND WAS ALSO ACCOMPANIED BY AT LEAST GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL WITH A REPORT FROM 5 NNW OF LIBERAL OF HAIL PENETRAING A ROOF.
